 {"id":"bd-r2n1","title":"Add integration tests for RPC server and event loops","description":"After adding basic unit tests for daemon utilities, the complex daemon functions still need integration tests:

Core daemon lifecycle:
- startRPCServer: Initializes and starts RPC server with proper error handling
- runEventLoop: Polling-based sync loop with parent monitoring and signal handling
- runDaemonLoop: Main daemon initialization and setup

Health checking:
- isDaemonHealthy: Checks daemon responsiveness and health metrics
- checkDaemonHealth: Periodic health verification

These require more complex test infrastructure (mock RPC, test contexts, signal handling) and should be tackled after the unit test foundation is in place.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T12:28:56.022996362-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T12:44:32.167862713-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T12:44:32.167862713-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r2n1","depends_on_id":"bd-4or","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-18T12:28:56.045893852-07:00","created_by":"mhwilkie"}]}

-{"id":"bd-r4sn","title":"Phase 2.5: TOON-based daemon sync","description":"Implement TOON-native daemon sync (replaces JSONL sync machinery).

## Overview
Daemon sync is the final integration point. Replace export/import/merge machinery with TOON-native sync, building on deletion tracking (2.3) and merge optimization (2.4).

## Required Work

### 2.5.1 TOON-based Daemon Sync
- [ ] Understand current JSONL sync machinery (export.go, import.go, merge.go)
- [ ] Replace export step with TOON encoding (EncodeTOON)
- [ ] Replace import step with TOON decoding (DecodeTOON)
- [ ] Replace merge step with TOON-aware 3-way merge
- [ ] Update daemon auto-sync to read/write TOON
- [ ] Verify 5-second debounce still works

### 2.5.2 Deletion Sync Integration
- [ ] Load deletions.toon during import phase
- [ ] Apply deletions after merging issues
- [ ] Ensure deletion TTL respects daemon schedule

### 2.5.3 Testing
- [ ] Unit tests for daemon sync with TOON
- [ ] Integration tests with actual daemon operations
- [ ] Multi-clone sync scenarios with concurrent edits
- [ ] Performance comparison with JSONL sync
- [ ] Long-running daemon stability tests

## Success Criteria
- Daemon reads/writes TOON format (not JSONL)
- Sync latency comparable to JSONL (<100ms)
- All 70+ tests passing
- bdt commands work seamlessly with daemon
- Multi-clone sync scenarios work correctly","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:20.33132177-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:20.33132177-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r4sn","depends_on_id":"bd-uz8r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:20.347724699-07:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-r4sn","depends_on_id":"bd-uwkp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:20.355379309-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

- Multi-clone sync scenarios work correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:20.33132177-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:25.274362-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:25.274362-08:00","close_reason":"TOON approach declined","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r4sn","depends_on_id":"bd-uz8r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:20.347724699-07:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-r4sn","depends_on_id":"bd-uwkp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:20.355379309-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-r6a","title":"Redesign workflow system: templates as native Beads","description":"## Problem

The current workflow system (YAML templates in cmd/bd/templates/workflows/) is architecturally flawed:

1. **Out-of-band data plane** - YAML files are a parallel system outside Beads itself
2. **Heavyweight DSL** - YAML is gross; even TOML would have been better, but neither is ideal
3. **Not graph-native** - Beads IS already a dependency graph with priorities, so why reinvent it?
4. **Can't use bd commands on templates** - They're opaque YAML, not viewable/editable Beads

## The Right Design

**Templates should be Beads themselves.**

A "workflow template" should be:
- An epic marked as a template (via label, type, or prefix like `tpl-`)
- Child issues with dependencies between them (using normal bd dep)
- Titles and descriptions containing `{{variable}}` placeholders
- Normal priorities that control serialization order

"Instantiation" becomes:
1. Clone the template subgraph (epic + children + dependencies)
2. Substitute variables in titles/descriptions
3. Generate new IDs for all cloned issues
4. Return the new epic ID

## Benefits

- **No YAML** - Templates are just Beads
- **Use existing tools** - `bd show`, `bd edit`, `bd dep` work on templates
- **Graph-native** - Dependencies are real Beads dependencies
- **Simpler codebase** - Remove all the YAML parsing/workflow code
- **Composable** - Templates can reference other templates

## Tasks

1. Delete the YAML workflow system code (revert recent push + remove existing workflow code)
2. Design template marking convention (label? type? id prefix?)
3. Implement `bd template create` or `bd clone --as-template`
4. Implement `bd template instantiate <template-id> --var key=value`
5. Migrate version-bump workflow to native Beads template
6. Update documentation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:41:57.359643-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T17:42:26.000769-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T13:47:04.632525-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-r6a.1","title":"Revert/remove YAML workflow system","description":"Revert the recent commit and remove all YAML workflow code:

1. `git revert aae8407a` (the commit we just pushed with workflow fixes)
2. Remove `cmd/bd/templates/workflows/` directory
3. Remove workflow.go or gut it to minimal stub
4. Remove WorkflowTemplate types from internal/types/workflow.go
5. Remove any workflow-related RPC handlers

Keep only minimal scaffolding if needed for the new template system.","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:42:07.339684-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T22:46:08.606088-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T22:46:08.606088-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r6a.1","depends_on_id":"bd-r6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:42:07.340117-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-r6a.2","title":"Implement subgraph cloning with variable substitution","description":"Core implementation of template instantiation:

1. Add `bd template instantiate <template-id> [--var key=value]...` command
2. Implement subgraph loading:
   - Load template epic
   - Recursively load all children (and their children)
   - Load all dependencies between issues in the subgraph
3. Implement variable substitution:
   - Scan titles and descriptions for `{{name}}` patterns
   - Replace with provided values
   - Error on missing required variables (or prompt interactively)
4. Implement cloning:
   - Generate new IDs for all issues
   - Create cloned issues with substituted text
   - Remap and create dependencies
5. Return the new epic ID

Consider adding `--dry-run` flag to preview what would be created.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:43:25.179848-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:02:29.034444-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:02:29.034444-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r6a.2","depends_on_id":"bd-r6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:43:25.180286-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-r6a.2","depends_on_id":"bd-r6a.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:44:03.15413-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"bd-usro","title":"Rename 'template instantiate' to 'mol bond'","description":"Rename the template instantiation command to match molecule metaphor.

Current: bd template instantiate <id> --var key=value
Target:  bd mol bond <id> --var key=value

Changes needed:
- Add 'mol' command group (or extend existing)
- Add 'bond' subcommand that wraps template instantiate logic
- Keep 'template instantiate' as deprecated alias for backward compat
- Update help text and docs to use molecule terminology

The 'bond' verb captures:
1. Chemistry metaphor (molecules bond to form structures)
2. Dependency linking (child issues bonded in a DAG)
3. Short and active

See also: molecule execution model in Gas Town","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T16:56:37.582795-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T23:22:43.567337-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T23:22:43.567337-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented mol command: catalog, show, bond"}

-{"id":"bd-uwkp","title":"Phase 2.4: Git merge driver optimization for TOON format","description":"Optimize git 3-way merge for TOON line-oriented format.

## Overview
TOON is line-oriented (unlike binary formats), enabling smarter git merge strategies. Implement custom merge driver to handle TOON-specific merge patterns.

## Required Work

### 2.4.1 TOON Merge Driver
- [ ] Create .git/info/attributes entry for *.toon files
- [ ] Implement custom merge driver script/command
- [ ] Handle tabular format row merges (line-based 3-way)
- [ ] Handle YAML-style format merges
- [ ] Conflict markers for unsolvable conflicts

### 2.4.2 Merge Patterns
- [ ] Row addition: both branches add different rows → union
- [ ] Row deletion: one branch deletes, other modifies → conflict (manual review)
- [ ] Row modification: concurrent field changes → intelligent merge or conflict
- [ ] Field ordering changes: ignore (TOON format resilient to order)

### 2.4.3 Testing & Documentation
- [ ] Unit tests for merge scenarios (3-way merge logic)
- [ ] Integration tests with actual git merges
- [ ] Conflict scenario testing
- [ ] Documentation of merge strategy

## Success Criteria
- Git merge handles TOON conflicts intelligently
- Fewer manual merge conflicts than JSONL
- Round-trip preserved through merges
- All 70+ tests still passing
- Git history stays clean (minimal conflict markers)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:14.339238776-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:14.339238776-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uwkp","depends_on_id":"bd-iic1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:14.34427988-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-uz8r","title":"Phase 2.3: TOON deletion tracking","description":"Implement deletion tracking in TOON format.

## Overview
Phase 2.2 switched storage to TOON format. Phase 2.3 adds deletion tracking in TOON format for propagating deletions across clones.

## Required Work

### 2.3.1 Deletion Tracking (TOON Format)
- [ ] Implement deletions.toon file (tracking deleted issue records)
- [ ] Add DeleteTracker struct to record deleted issue IDs and metadata
- [ ] Update bdt delete command to record in deletions.toon
- [ ] Design deletion record format (ID, timestamp, reason, hash)
- [ ] Implement auto-prune of old deletion records (configurable TTL)

### 2.3.2 Sync Propagation
- [ ] Load deletions.toon during import
- [ ] Remove deleted issues from local database when imported from remote
- [ ] Handle edge cases (delete same issue in multiple clones)
- [ ] Deletion ordering and conflict resolution

### 2.3.3 Testing
- [ ] Unit tests for deletion tracking
- [ ] Integration tests for deletion propagation
- [ ] Multi-clone deletion scenarios
- [ ] TTL expiration tests

## Success Criteria
- deletions.toon stores deletion records in TOON format
- Deletions propagate across clones via git sync
- Old records auto-prune after TTL
- All 70+ tests still passing
- bdt delete command works seamlessly","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:37:23.722066816-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:07.697460918-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uz8r","depends_on_id":"bd-iic1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:37:23.726825771-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

+{"id":"bd-uz8r","title":"Phase 2.3: TOON deletion tracking","description":"Implement deletion tracking in TOON format.

## Overview
Phase 2.2 switched storage to TOON format. Phase 2.3 adds deletion tracking in TOON format for propagating deletions across clones.

## Required Work

### 2.3.1 Deletion Tracking (TOON Format)
- [ ] Implement deletions.toon file (tracking deleted issue records)
- [ ] Add DeleteTracker struct to record deleted issue IDs and metadata
- [ ] Update bdt delete command to record in deletions.toon
- [ ] Design deletion record format (ID, timestamp, reason, hash)
- [ ] Implement auto-prune of old deletion records (configurable TTL)

### 2.3.2 Sync Propagation
- [ ] Load deletions.toon during import
- [ ] Remove deleted issues from local database when imported from remote
- [ ] Handle edge cases (delete same issue in multiple clones)
- [ ] Deletion ordering and conflict resolution

### 2.3.3 Testing
- [ ] Unit tests for deletion tracking
- [ ] Integration tests for deletion propagation
- [ ] Multi-clone deletion scenarios
- [ ] TTL expiration tests

## Success Criteria
- deletions.toon stores deletion records in TOON format
- Deletions propagate across clones via git sync
- Old records auto-prune after TTL
- All 70+ tests still passing
- bdt delete command works seamlessly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:37:23.722066816-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:27.491932-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:27.491932-08:00","close_reason":"TOON approach declined","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uz8r","depends_on_id":"bd-iic1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:37:23.726825771-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
